title: rubia peregrina var. requienii
intro: Rubia peregrina var. requienii, commonly known as Creeping Madder or Wild Madder, is a perennial plant species within the Rubiaceae family, native to the Mediterranean region. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties, making it effective in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Creeping Madder include reducing inflammation and pain, improving skin conditions, and supporting the immune system. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is often combined with other herbs in traditional medicine to enhance its therapeutic effects.
